title: aster lateriflorus var. spatelliformis
intro: Aster lateriflorus var. spatelliformis, also known as the white wood's aster or lateral wood's aster, is a flowering plant that is a variant of the wood's aster, characterized by its white or pale pink flowers. This plant is believed to have medicinal properties, primarily due to its saponin and flavonoid content, which may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Aster lateriflorus var. spatelliformis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. The herbal preparation of this plant typically involves drying and infusing the flowers in hot water or using them in tinctures and salves to harness its medicinal properties.
